LAW AND MOTION 302, (1) PLAINTIFF JOHN LIGGETT'S DEMURRER TO CROSS-COMPLAINT AND (2) PLAINTIFF'S ALTERNATIVE MOTION TO STRIKE - SUSTAIN DEMURRER, WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO BOTH CAUSES OF ACTION. THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR DECLARATORY RELIEF RAISES A QUESTION OF FACT THAT WILL NECESSARILY BE SETTLED IN THE UNDERLYING COMPLAINT. IT FAILS TO STATE A CLAIM UNDER CCP 430.10(E) AND CCP 1060 AND 1061. THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR CREDIT/SET OFF IS AN AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSE THAT HAS ALREADY BEEN RAISED IN RESPONSE TO THE ORIGINAL COMPLAINT. (SEE THIRD AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSE.) CCP 430.10(E). CROSS-COMPLAINANT DOES NOT OPPOSE THE MOTION TO STRIKE THE REQUEST FOR ATTORNEY FEES. HOWEVER, IN LIGHT OF THE RULING ON THE DEMURRER, THE MOTION TO STRIKE IS DENIED AS MOOT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185 =302/MJM

DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-20-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
(1) PLAINTIFF JOHN LIGGETT'S DEMURRER TO CROSS-COMPLAINT AND (2) PLAINTIFF'S ALTERNATIVE MOTION TO STRIKE - Sustain demurrer, without leave to amend as to both causes of action. The first cause of action for declaratory relief raises a question of fact that will necessarily be settled in the underlying Complaint. It fails to state a claim under CCP 430.10(e) and CCP 1060 and 1061. The second cause of action for credit/set off is an affirmative defense that has already been raised in response to the original Complaint. (See Third Affirmative Defense.) CCP 430.10(e). Cross-Complainant does not oppose the motion to strike the request for attorney fees. However, in light of the ruling on the demurrer, the motion to strike is denied as moot. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =302/mjm

DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-27-2013 Continued To Sep-20-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
